name: Bean Biscuits
recipeInstructions:
Revised after testing February 1994. Used 1 1/2 cups flour instead of the 2 1/2 cups printed in the original book. The 1 cup beans likely replaced 1 cup flour in the original recipe.
1. Stir together dry ingredients in a mixing bowl.
2. Cut in butter until mixture resembles crumbs.
3. Puree beans and milk in a food processor or blender, or mash beans and stir in milk.
4. Mix all at once to form a light, soft dough.
5. Turn out onto lightly floured surface, sprinkle with flour, and knead for about 20 seconds.
6. Shape biscuits by rolling, patting, and cutting; or divide dough into 16 pieces and shape by hand.
7. Bake at 400°F (200°C) for 12 to 15 minutes or until golden brown.
Note: Each biscuit contains approximately 162 calories, 1.5 starch units, and 1 fats & oils exchange, with 4 grams total fat, 0.8 grams saturated fat, no cholesterol, 5 grams protein, 26 grams carbohydrate, 169 mg sodium, and 204 mg potassium. Adapted from "Full of Beans" by V. Currie & Kay Spicer, 1993; shared and tested by Elizabeth Rodier in February 1994.
